description: The visual quality that distinguishes a red flower from a blue one is 'welisha'. A source noun can stand before it: 'kerou welisha' (stone color) is gray. The registered compound 'haoni welisha' (voice color) carries the same image into timbre.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then unround into true-mid front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and move into stressed high-front 'i'. Raise the tongue blade toward the postalveolar region for voiceless 'sh', then open into central 'a'.
usage_notes: As a noun, 'welisha' takes a source before it. This pattern covers shades outside the seven adjective roots; 'horathe welisha' is dawn color, pink.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: the visual category used to name and compare colors
semantic_domains.physical: a perceived property of surfaces or light
